days.world_headline: The Hormuz truce collapses overnight — Iran shuts the world's oil gate 'until further notice,' fires on a container ship and strikes five Gulf states while the US pounds 140 Iranian targets — and India wakes on a calm Friday close that markets will have to reprice on Monday.
days.market_headline: The risk we flagged just happened: the Hormuz truce collapsed over the weekend — Iran shut the strait and struck Gulf states — so Friday's calm close (oil $71, VIX 15, Sensex +1%) now faces a Monday repricing, with Bitcoin's -0.5% weekend slip the only live tell.

days.world_headline: The Hormuz war cools into a war of words — Iran calls its tanker strike 'a mistake' and its foreign minister flies to Oman to guarantee safe passage, oil slides to ~$71, and India rides the relief to a second straight rally even as Modi seals a landmark New Zealand partnership and a Vietnam boat tragedy kills 15 Indian tourists.
days.market_headline: The relief holds: oil slides to ~$71 as the Hormuz war turns to talks, India rallies a second day (Sensex +827), Nvidia breaks out +4% — but foreigners keep quietly selling India.

days.world_headline: The US-Iran war grinds on — Khamenei is buried, fresh strikes hit southern Iran and Hormuz shipping halts — yet the market panic is already fading, as oil settles back toward $72, the fear gauge collapses, and India snaps back with a 700-point rally after TCS beats forecasts.
days.market_headline: Relief rally: oil settles near $72 despite the Hormuz halt, the VIX crashes, and a TCS earnings beat powers India's Sensex up ~700 points a day after its worst crash in months.

days.world_headline: The war everyone hoped was over came back overnight: after Iranian forces hit three tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, the US struck more than 80 targets across Iran and 60-plus Revolutionary Guard boats, Iran fired back at American bases in Bahrain and Kuwait, and Trump declared the ceasefire 'over' and said the US will 'probably hit them hard again tonight' — sending oil toward $80, halting tanker traffic through the world's key oil gate, crashing Indian stocks 1,677 points and sinking the rupee, all as Iran buries Khamenei in Mashhad with his unseen successor barred from his own father's funeral and TCS opens India's earnings season into the storm.
days.market_headline: War risk goes live: the US and Iran trade strikes, oil rockets toward $80 and Hormuz shipping halts — Indian stocks suffer their worst day in months, the rupee hits a record low, and TCS opens earnings season tonight into the storm.

days.world_headline: The risk everyone was watching finally broke: Iranian projectiles hit a Qatari gas tanker and a Saudi supertanker in the Strait of Hormuz, shattering the fragile US–Iran truce, spiking oil and reviving the one fear that hurts India most — while NATO haggles in Ankara, a French court hands Marine Le Pen a lifeline, and Indian markets snap a winning streak the day before TCS opens earnings season.
days.market_headline: Risk-off flips the script: an attack on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz sent oil surging ~3%, AI chips got 'pummeled' overnight, and India's five-day rally snapped — all one day before TCS opens earnings season.

days.world_headline: Summit season meets the ground truth: NATO leaders gather in Ankara to fund a war that just showed Ukraine can't defend its own skies, France's top court decides today whether Marine Le Pen can run for president, Iran readies Khamenei's burial with its new leader still unseen — while India turns diplomacy into hardware, sealing a BrahMos-missile pact in Jakarta and watching five of its states cross a World Bank income line.
days.market_headline: Risk-on returns: Wall Street reopened to a record Dow and 'revived AI optimism', and India's IT stocks led a fifth straight up-day ahead of TCS's results — while oil bounced off oversold lows and the rupee steadied near ~95.3.

days.world_headline: Diplomacy on the phone, escalation on the ground: two days after Trump called Putin to talk peace, Russia hit Kyiv with its heaviest missile-and-drone barrage yet, exposing a dangerous hole in Ukraine's air defences — as Iran's vengeance-charged funeral for Khamenei rolls on, Hamas hands its Gaza government to a technocratic committee, and falling oil powers a fourth-straight-day rally in Indian stocks.
days.market_headline: Falling oil did the heavy lifting: India's Sensex and Nifty closed higher a fourth straight day led by banks, while the rupee slipped to a record-low ~95.4 — and the US reopens tonight into a Fed-minutes week, with Bitcoin's weekend bounce already failing.

days.world_headline: The world's oil arteries are the story of the day — the Strait of Hormuz reopens as a wounded Iran buries Khamenei, while Ukraine sets a Russian oil terminal ablaze and Trump phones Putin to talk peace.
days.market_headline: A quiet Sunday with the tape still frozen at last week's closes — but the big change is relief: the Strait of Hormuz is reopening, India has stood down its gas emergency, and the week ahead turns on Indian Q1 earnings, not an oil shock.

days.world_headline: America turns 250 under a deadly heat dome while Iran's threat to charge tolls in the Strait of Hormuz turns oil into the world's biggest wildcard.
days.market_headline: A holiday-weekend lull with the tape frozen at last week's closes — but Iran's Strait of Hormuz toll threat leaves oil (and India) the thing to watch, while Bitcoin quietly firms.

days.world_headline: Russia unleashes its biggest strike yet on Kyiv as Iran buries its assassinated Supreme Leader — while a weak US jobs report quietly flips the world's most important interest-rate question.
days.market_headline: A weak US jobs report kills the rate-hike fear — Dow hits a record and gold jumps, Apple rockets ~5%, while India grinds higher against a soft rupee and record foreign outflows.

days.world_headline: The Fed's new chair strikes a calmer tone as inflation expectations ease — cooling yesterday's rate-hike panic, even as the AI trade wobbles and India's tech stocks roar back.
days.market_headline: Fed's Warsh calms the rate-hike fear as inflation expectations ease — US tech wobbles on AI doubts while India's IT stocks lead a rebound.

days.world_headline: A Middle East energy shock and a fresh inflation scare push the Fed toward rate HIKES — and it reaches all the way to India's pump prices and the rupee.
days.market_headline: Risk-on tape shrugs off a hot core PCE print as AI names lead — but Bitcoin sits deeply oversold near 52-week lows.
